description: Migration Fix-Up: Export Sanity dataset, audit gaps vs BaseHub, fix schemas (add title fields for Studio display), and create new standalone fix scripts to fill all missing/broken content

userStories.title: Export Sanity dataset and create audit script
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to export the current Sanity dataset and build an audit script that parses the NDJSON locally to report exactly what's missing, broken, or incomplete — without hitting the Sanity API.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npx sanity@latest dataset export production scripts/migration/data/production-export.tar.gz` to download the dataset
Create `scripts/migration/audit-dataset.ts` that extracts and parses the NDJSON from the export
Audit script counts documents per _type and compares against expected BaseHub counts
Audit script lists documents with missing required fields (title, slug, _type)
Audit script lists all broken references (documents referencing _ids that don't exist in the dataset)
Audit script lists documents with empty portable text fields (intro, content, description) that should have content
Audit script outputs a summary table: type | expected | actual | missing | broken_refs
Add `scripts/migration/data/` to .gitignore
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The export tarball contains NDJSON. Parse it with ndjson or line-by-line JSON.parse. This gives us ground truth without API calls. Expected counts come from BaseHub queries run in the same script.

userStories.title: Add title fields to singleton schemas for Studio display
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to add a title field to singleton schemas that are missing one, and configure all document schemas to use the correct field as the Studio display title, so Sanity Studio shows readable names instead of IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add hidden `title` field with a default value to singletons that lack one: homepage (default 'Homepage'), servicesPage (default 'Services'), companyInfo (default 'Company Info'), threeDAssets (default '3D Assets')
peoplePage already has a title field — keep it as-is
careersPostPage already has heroTitle — add a hidden `title` field with default 'Careers'
Add `preview: { select: { title: 'title' } }` to all document schemas that don't already have it
For document types where the display field is not 'title' (e.g. testimonial uses 'name'), set preview.select.title to the correct field
Run `npx sanity@latest schema deploy` to push updated schemas
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BaseHub gives every document a _title by default. Sanity doesn't — schemas need explicit title fields or preview config. Hidden title fields on singletons ensure Studio sidebar shows names.

userStories.title: Audit and fix BaseHub query for clients
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to fix the client migration so all 41 clients are created in Sanity. The previous migration created 0 clients because the BaseHub query treated logo as an image object instead of a string URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Read `scripts/migration/migrate-company.ts` and verify the BaseHub query for clients fetches logo correctly (logo is a string URL, not an object with url/alt)

Create `scripts/migration/fix-clients.ts` as a standalone script that: fetches all clients from BaseHub, creates client documents in Sanity with deterministic _id (client-{slug}), downloads and uploads logo images, uses createOrReplace
Script handles cases where logo is null/undefined (creates client without logo)
Script logs each client created with success/failure
Running `pnpm exec tsx scripts/migration/fix-clients.ts` creates all 41 clients
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Root cause was logo field type mismatch. The fix script should be robust — try multiple query shapes if the first fails. Log the raw BaseHub response for the first client to verify field structure.

userStories.title: Create missing person documents for former team members
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to create person documents for former team members who appear as blog post authors but are not in the current people directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-missing-persons.ts` that queries BaseHub for ALL post authors across all blog posts
Script compares BaseHub author slugs against existing Sanity person documents
Script creates person documents for any authors not found in Sanity, using: _id: person-{slug}, _type: person, title: {author name from BaseHub}
Known missing persons include: person-basement-team, person-natalia-martinez-lopez, person-matias-perez, person-joaquin-montes, person-agustin-segui, person-julian-benegas
Script discovers any additional missing persons dynamically (not hardcoded list only)
Uses createOrReplace for idempotency
Running `pnpm exec tsx scripts/migration/fix-missing-persons.ts` creates all missing persons
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Don't hardcode the list — query BaseHub to discover all authors and diff against Sanity. Some former team members may have images in BaseHub that should be uploaded.

userStories.title: Fix and re-migrate all projects with client references
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to re-run the project migration now that clients exist, ensuring all 31 projects and 25 showcase entries are created with valid client references.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-projects.ts` as a standalone script
Script fetches all projects from BaseHub and creates/replaces them in Sanity
Every project document has valid client reference (client must exist — verify before setting ref, skip ref if client missing)
Every project has valid people references (verify each person exists)
Downloads and uploads cover images, icons, and showcase item images
Creates showcaseEntry documents for all showcase entries with valid project references
Uses createOrReplace for idempotency
Logs summary: X projects created, Y showcase entries created, Z references skipped
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (clients exist) and US-004 (all persons exist). Must verify references exist before including them to avoid Sanity reference validation errors.

userStories.title: Fix rich text converter for post validation errors
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to fix the portable text conversion so all blog posts pass Sanity validation. 12 posts failed with 'Bad Request - Validation failed' which means the PT structure doesn't match the schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Read `scripts/migration/utils/rich-text.ts` and the post schema at `sanity/schemas/documents/post.ts`
Read the content field definition to understand exactly which block types are allowed in content vs intro
Ensure every PT block has: _type, _key (unique string), children array (for text blocks), markDefs array (for text blocks)
Ensure every span in children has: _type: 'span', _key, text (string), marks (array of strings)
Ensure markDefs entries have: _type, _key, and match mark references in spans
Ensure custom block types (_type: codeBlock, quoteWithAuthor, etc.) match their Sanity schema field names exactly
Add a sanitizeDocument function that validates the full post document before sending to Sanity — log and strip any invalid blocks rather than failing the whole post
Intro field must only contain basic block types (no custom blocks, no images)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Compare the _type values in converted PT against the `of` array in the post content schema. Any _type not listed in the schema will cause validation failure. Also check for empty children arrays or missing text properties.

userStories.title: Re-migrate all blog posts
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to re-run the post migration with the fixed rich text converter and missing persons, ensuring all 27 posts are created.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-posts.ts` as a standalone script
Script fetches all 27 posts from BaseHub
Converts intro and content rich text using the fixed converter
Validates all author references exist before including them
Downloads and uploads hero images
Creates post documents with createOrReplace
Every post document passes Sanity validation (no 'Bad Request' errors)
Logs per-post success/failure with detailed error info for any failures
All 27 posts created successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (missing persons) and US-006 (fixed rich text). If a post still fails, log the exact error and the problematic field content, then skip it rather than crashing.

userStories.title: Fix testimonials BaseHub query and migrate
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to fix the testimonials migration that crashed with 'type Testimonials does not have a field items' and migrate all testimonials.
acceptanceCriteria: Read BaseHub schema types to discover the correct field name for testimonials (not 'items')
Create `scripts/migration/fix-testimonials.ts` that fetches testimonials from the correct BaseHub path
Creates testimonial documents in Sanity with: name, handle, content, avatar (uploaded), date, role
Uses createOrReplace for idempotency
Logs count of testimonials created
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The BaseHub type is 'Testimonials' but the field is NOT 'items'. Check .basehub/ generated types or query explorer to find the correct field. Could be 'testimonialList' or similar.

userStories.title: Fix values migration and migrate
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to ensure company values are migrated. The values step was in migrate-services.ts which crashed during testimonials, so values were never reached.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-values.ts` that fetches values from company.ourValues.valuesList in BaseHub
Creates value documents in Sanity with: title, description (converted PT), image (uploaded)
Uses createOrReplace for idempotency
Logs count of values created
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Values migration code may already exist in migrate-services.ts but never executed due to crash. Can reuse that logic in standalone script.

userStories.title: Re-migrate awards with valid project references
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to re-run awards migration now that all projects exist, so awards can reference their projects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-awards.ts` that fetches all 30 awards from BaseHub
Verifies project references exist before including them (skip ref if project missing, don't crash)
Downloads and uploads certificate images
Creates award documents with createOrReplace
All 30 awards created (12 existed + 18 that previously failed)
Logs summary with success/failure count
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (all projects exist). 18 awards failed because they referenced missing projects.

userStories.title: Re-migrate homepage singleton with valid project references
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to re-create the homepage singleton now that projects exist, so featuredProjects references are valid.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-homepage.ts` that fetches homepage data from BaseHub
Converts introTitle, introSubtitle, capabilities via rich text converter
Sets featuredProjects as references to existing project documents (verify each exists)
Creates homepage document with _id: 'homepage' and createOrReplace
Sets title field to 'Homepage' (hidden, for Studio display)
Homepage document created successfully with all fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (projects exist). Only 1 singleton failed — the others (servicesPage, peoplePage, careersPostPage, companyInfo) already migrated.

userStories.title: Create fix-all orchestrator and run full verification
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need to create an orchestrator that runs all fix scripts in dependency order, then runs a full verification comparing Sanity against BaseHub.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/fix-all.ts` that runs fix scripts in order: fix-clients → fix-missing-persons → fix-projects → fix-posts → fix-testimonials → fix-values → fix-awards → fix-homepage
Each step logs start/end with timing
Errors in one step don't block subsequent steps
After all fixes, runs the audit-dataset logic (or verify.ts) to produce a final report
Final report shows: all document types have expected counts, zero broken references, zero missing required fields
Script is runnable via `pnpm exec tsx scripts/migration/fix-all.ts`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the single command to run everything. After this completes with a clean report, migration Phase 2 is done.
